commands.review_summary:
The PR changes cron channel output policy so unresolved/no-channel cron delivery prefers final assistant text and adds a regression test for the undefined-channel case.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +7. Total +8 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a bare `delivery.mode: "none"` cron run produces `resolvedDelivery.channel: undefined`, current main returns `preferFinalAssistantVisibleText: false`, and the recovery gate requires that flag to be true. I did not run the live cron scenario because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the PR already contains the narrow code/test fix and needs normal CI or maintainer landing review.

target.title: fix(cron): no-deliver runs now recover from tool warnings via final assistant text
target.body:
### Summary

- `resolveCronChannelOutputPolicy(undefined)` previously returned `preferFinalAssistantVisibleText: false` for no-deliver cron runs (where `resolvedDelivery.channel` is `undefined`).
- Both `hasRecoveredToolWarning` and `shouldUseFinalAssistantVisibleText` in `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` gate on `preferFinalAssistantVisibleText === true`, so no-deliver runs could never use final assistant text to recover from non-fatal tool warnings — they were always marked fatal.
- When there is no channel, there is no structured delivery; the final assistant text is the only meaningful output for run status and ledger recording. The fix returns `true` for the no-channel path.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `--no-deliver` cron runs that produce final assistant text after only non-fatal tool warnings are incorrectly marked as fatal errors and record an error payload instead of the assistant answer.

**Real environment tested:** Local source inspection on `origin/main` (`afa04d6454f5`); source-level path traced end-to-end (`run.ts:385` → `resolveCronChannelOutputPolicy(undefined)` → `helpers.ts:289,312`).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/channel-output-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** 3 + 60 + 7 = 70 tests pass, 0 failures.

**Observed result after fix:** New test `"prefers final assistant text when no channel is resolved"` asserts `resolveCronChannelOutputPolicy(undefined)` resolves to `{ preferFinalAssistantVisibleText: true }` and that the channel plugin runtime is never consulted (function returns before the lazy import).

**What was not tested:** Live gateway cron run with `--no-deliver` + tool warning; acceptance criteria tests in ClawSweeper review require `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/channel-output-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts`.

### Reproduction

1. Configure a cron job with `--no-deliver` (or `deliveryPlan.mode = "none"` with no explicit target).
2. Run an agent that emits only tool-warning payloads followed by a valid final assistant-visible reply.

**Before this PR:** `hasFatalErrorPayload = true`; run recorded as error; final assistant text discarded.

**After this PR:** `hasRecoveredToolWarning = true`; `hasFatalErrorPayload = false`; final assistant text used as run output.

### Risk / Mitigation

- The downstream guards in `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` (`!runLevelError`, `failureSignal?.fatalForCron !== true`, `!hasStructuredDeliveryPayloads`) remain unchanged — the flag alone cannot suppress real failures, failure signals, or structured payloads.
- No config surface, plugin SDK, or protocol change.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] cron

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90664
